Assembles context (history, file contents, MCP tools, code index), streams response chunks to renderer, parses XML action tags mid-stream to write files and install deps, saves full SDK message objects to SQLite for multi-turn continuity.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/codegen/study/ai-chat-stream--from-dyad.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/codegen/build/ai-chat-stream--from-dyad.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/dyad-sh/dyad)

- **Design Artifact Generation** — from open-design. 4 pathways: HTML prototypes (inline-CSS React, sandboxed iframe), HTML decks (fixed skeleton + SLOT: fills), images (AIHubMix), and MP4 videos (Seedance/Wan/Veo/Sora). Async polling for long video renders.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/codegen/study/design-artifact-generation--from-open-design.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/codegen/build/design-artifact-generation--from-open-design.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/nexu-io/open-design)

- **Image Generation** — from dyad. In-builder AI image generation: 4 style-theme prompt prefixes, AbortController map for cancellation, 120s timeout, handles b64_json and URL responses, HTTPS-only URL validation, 50MB cap, file-locked write to .dyad/media/, returns relative path for AI code references.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/codegen/study/image-generation--from-dyad.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/codegen/build/image-generation--from-dyad.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/dyad-sh/dyad)

- **Code Explorer** — from dyad. TypeScript-compiler-backed codebase indexer in worker threads. Discovers tsconfig in monorepos by scoring dir names, extracts exported symbols with line numbers, ranks against query via stemming+architecture heuristics, injects top symbols into AI system prompt.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/codegen/study/code-explorer--from-dyad.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/codegen/build/code-explorer--from-dyad.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/dyad-sh/dyad)

### Document Conversion (4)
Converting structured documents (PDFs, Office files, archives, plaintext) into clean Markdown for downstream text analysis or LLM consumption.

- **Converter Pipeline** — from markitdown. Priority-sorted pluggable converter registry. Source-type dispatch → layered file detection → sorted converter chain. First accepts+succeeds wins. Output normalized to clean Markdown.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/document-conversion/study/converter-pipeline--from-markitdown.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/document-conversion/build/converter-pipeline--from-markitdown.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/microsoft/markitdown)

- **Office Doc Conversion** — from markitdown. DOCX/PPTX/XLSX → Markdown via python-docx, python-pptx, pandas. Tables go HTML→Markdown via HtmlConverter.convert_string(). PPTX handles charts, speaker notes, and optional LLM image captions.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/document-conversion/study/office-doc-conversion--from-markitdown.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/document-conversion/build/office-doc-conversion--from-markitdown.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/microsoft/markitdown)

- **PDF Conversion** — from markitdown. Dual-library PDF extraction: pdfplumber primary (table/form column detection, page.close() memory management) → pdfminer fallback. Markdown tables from column-clustered word positions.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/document-conversion/study/pdf-conversion--from-markitdown.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/document-conversion/build/pdf-conversion--from-markitdown.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/microsoft/markitdown)

- **ZIP Archive Traversal** — from markitdown. Recursive ZIP extraction: iterate entries → BytesIO streams → call parent pipeline on each file. Silent skip on conversion failure. Nested ZIPs handled automatically.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/document-conversion/study/zip-archive-traversal--from-markitdown.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/document-conversion/build/zip-archive-traversal--from-markitdown.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/microsoft/markitdown)

### Content Preprocessing (3)
Reducing raw, messy source material into a clean, compact representation an LLM can read cheaply and accurately — before any extraction or reasoning happens.

- **Page Format Pipeline** — from llm-scraper. preprocess(page,options) → {url,content,format}. Six formats: raw_html, markdown (Turndown), text (Mozilla Readability via CDN in-page), html (cleanup scrub, default), image (base64 screenshot), custom. Cost/fidelity dial.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/content-preprocessing/study/page-format-pipeline--from-llm-scraper.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/content-preprocessing/build/page-format-pipeline--from-llm-scraper.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/mishushakov/llm-scraper)

- **PDF Ingestion Pipeline** — from openpaper. Two-service webhook-driven background ingest: server validates+stages to S3, Celery worker extracts markdown (markitdown->pymupdf4llm) + page offsets, renders preview, runs 4 parallel Gemini structured-output calls (shared context cache), POSTs results to a webhook that enriches a placeholder Paper row.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/content-preprocessing/study/pdf-ingestion-pipeline--from-openpaper.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/content-preprocessing/build/pdf-ingestion-pipeline--from-openpaper.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/khoj-ai/openpaper)

- **HTML Cleanup** — from llm-scraper. In-browser DOM scrub via page.evaluate. Removes ~30 tag types (script/style/media/form/nav/head) and prefix-strips noise attributes (style/src/on*/aria-/data-). Mutates live DOM. href kept. Token-diet for the default html format.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/content-preprocessing/study/html-cleanup--from-llm-scraper.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/content-preprocessing/build/html-cleanup--from-llm-scraper.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/mishushakov/llm-scraper)

### Content Synthesis (3)
Turning raw multi-source retrieval results into coherent, deduplicated, ranked summaries — clustering duplicate stories, selecting diverse representatives, and generating shareable output formats.

- **Generate llms.txt** — from firecrawl. Auto-generate a site's llms.txt (titled page list + one-line descriptions) and optional llms-full.txt (full markdown, delimiter-separated) by composing map -> scrape -> per-page one-line-summary LLM. Async job, Redis status, Supabase cache, entry/page caps.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/content-synthesis/study/generate-llms-txt--from-firecrawl.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/content-synthesis/build/generate-llms-txt--from-firecrawl.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/firecrawl/firecrawl)

- **AI Carousel Generation** — from carousel-generator. OpenAI gpt-4o-mini forced to call one function whose params are a Zod content schema (zodToJsonSchema). Styled/unstyled split: LLM fills content only, styling defaults merged via Zod .default(). safeParse gate, temp 0.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/content-synthesis/study/ai-carousel-generation--from-carousel-generator.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/content-synthesis/build/ai-carousel-generation--from-carousel-generator.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/FranciscoMoretti/carousel-generator)

- **Cross-Source Clustering** — from last30days-skill. Two-pass clustering: Pass 1 greedy text similarity (0.42/0.48 thresholds), Pass 2 entity overlap on small clusters. MMR picks up to 3 reps per cluster. Polymarket isolation, same-source enforcement.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/content-synthesis/study/cross-source-clustering--from-last30days-skill.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/content-synthesis/build/cross-source-clustering--from-last30days-skill.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/mvanhorn/last30days-skill)

### Data Portability (3)
Getting data out of (and bulk into) a system in open, human-friendly formats — the "no lock-in" promise made real, plus ad-hoc reporting via spreadsheets.

- **Airbyte Protocol** — from airbyte. Universal source<->destination contract: a connector is a CLI emitting newline-delimited JSON AirbyteMessages (tagged union) over stdout. Commands spec/check/discover/read; read interleaves RECORD + STATE (resumable checkpoints) with LOG/TRACE/CONTROL. Language-agnostic (Docker + stdout); the platform persists STATE.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/data-portability/study/airbyte-protocol--from-airbyte.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/data-portability/build/airbyte-protocol--from-airbyte.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/airbytehq/airbyte)

- **CSV Import / Export** — from auto-crm. Partial-success JSON bulk import (201/207 + per-row errors, name required) and dual contacts/deals CSV export with correct escaping, UTF-8 BOM for Excel, humanized values, attachment download with dated filename.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/data-portability/study/csv-import-export--from-auto-crm.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/data-portability/build/csv-import-export--from-auto-crm.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/Hainrixz/auto-crm)

- **Zod Form Persistence & JSON Portability** — from carousel-generator. Server-less document model: auto-persist RHF doc to localStorage, validate-on-read and self-heal (clear+defaults on schema drift), and per-field JSON import/export validated by the matching Zod sub-schema before setValue.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/data-portability/study/zod-form-persistence--from-carousel-generator.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/data-portability/build/zod-form-persistence--from-carousel-generator.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/FranciscoMoretti/carousel-generator)

### Dev Tooling (3)
Patterns and infrastructure for developer workflow automation — parallel builds, environment reproducibility, agent-compatible project layouts, and CI/CD scaffolding.

- **Parallel Worktree Build** — from ai-website-cloner-template. git worktree add creates one isolated checkout per AI agent on a separate branch. Agents build non-overlapping page sections concurrently with no merge conflicts. Pre-written page.tsx slot pattern prevents the assembly file from being a conflict hotspot. Branches merged sequentially after all agents finish.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/dev-tooling/study/parallel-worktree-build--from-ai-website-cloner-template.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/dev-tooling/build/parallel-worktree-build--from-ai-website-cloner-template.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/JCodesMore/ai-website-cloner-template)

- **Dependency Manager** — from dyad. Auto npm installer triggered by AI-emitted <dyad-add-dependency> XML tags. Validates package names via regex, selects npm/pnpm, optionally routes through Socket Firewall, executes install with timeout, replaces tag in stored DB message with result tag.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/dev-tooling/study/dependency-manager--from-dyad.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/dev-tooling/build/dependency-manager--from-dyad.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/dyad-sh/dyad)

- **Agent Dashboard** — from serena. Flask REST API + SPA dashboard running in a background thread alongside the agent. In-memory log ring-buffer, tool call stats, config/memory/task CRUD. Platform-adaptive display: browser (Linux/macOS), pywebview native window (Windows), or system-tray aggregator for multiple instances.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/dev-tooling/study/agent-dashboard--from-serena.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/dev-tooling/build/agent-dashboard--from-serena.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/oraios/serena)

### Payments (3)
Everything involved in taking money from a buyer and getting it to a seller through a payment gateway — choosing the right provider, building the checkout/webhook/refund flows, and doing it securely (signed webhooks, idempotent charges, no leaked keys). This domain spans two very different concerns that always travel together:

- **Credit Billing & Concurrency** — from firecrawl. Metering: checkTeamCredits gates work from a cached balance chunk; billTeam decrements (Supabase RPC) non-blocking; sub-jobs self-bill. Concurrency via Redis sorted-sets: over-limit jobs parked in a queue, released as actives finish under plan/crawl maxConcurrency; expiry-scored actives swept.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/payments/study/credit-billing-and-concurrency--from-firecrawl.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/payments/build/credit-billing-and-concurrency--from-firecrawl.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/firecrawl/firecrawl)

- **Payment Provider Advisor** — from pagokit. Data-driven gateway selection: providers.json catalog with context-keyed score_modifiers. ≤5 questions -> hard filters (region/currency/methods/capability/KYC eliminate) -> score = base 5 + matching modifiers -> top pick. Recommendation gives reasons, real fee calc, KYC timeline, anti-patterns, 'verified on <date>'; never shows the score. Adding a provider = adding a JSON row.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/payments/study/payment-provider-advisor--from-pagokit.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/payments/build/payment-provider-advisor--from-pagokit.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/Hainrixz/agente-pagokit)

- **Secure Payment Webhook & Idempotency** — from pagokit. Correct payment integration shape: checkout with crypto.randomUUID() idempotency key; webhook in strict order raw body -> verify signature -> reject stale timestamp (replay, ~300s) -> dedup by event id -> act -> 200. Parse-before-verify is the core bug. Per-provider scheme read from data. 5 tables incl. webhook_events_processed. Enforced by the deterministic hooks.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/payments/study/secure-payment-webhook--from-pagokit.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/payments/build/secure-payment-webhook--from-pagokit.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/Hainrixz/agente-pagokit)

### State Management (3)
how an application holds, mutates, queries, and observes its in-memory state — especially structured document / editor state made of many interrelated objects that change constantly (often at 60fps during a drag).

- **Reactive Store Architecture** — from xyflow. Zustand store + selector/equality subscriptions for surgical re-renders; array<->Map duality (user nodes[] vs derived nodeLookup of enriched internal nodes); framework-free @xyflow/system core with thin React/Svelte adapters.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/state-management/study/reactive-store--from-xyflow.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/state-management/build/reactive-store--from-xyflow.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/xyflow/xyflow)

- **Change-Based Mutation Model** — from penpot. Every edit is a serializable `change` (tagged by :type, ~40 types) applied via a process-change dispatch table. Each forward change is built with its inverse (redo vector / undo list), so undo/redo, persistence, and realtime collab all replay the same change list. Event sourcing for a design file.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/state-management/study/change-based-mutation-model--from-penpot.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/state-management/build/change-based-mutation-model--from-penpot.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/penpot/penpot)

- **Reactive Record Store** — from tldraw. In-memory normalized record DB on signals. Flat 'type:id' records with scope (document/session/presence); reactive queries with incrementally-maintained indexes; every write emits a RecordsDiff {added,updated,removed} tagged source user|remote. Snapshots + migration on load.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/state-management/study/reactive-record-store--from-tldraw.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/state-management/build/reactive-record-store--from-tldraw.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/tldraw/tldraw)

### Agent Distribution (2)
Patterns for shipping, installing, and maintaining software through an AI agent — where a versioned natural-language runbook is the installer, bounded by explicit safety guardrails, and the same mechanism handles updates and ongoing health.

- **Agent-Driven Install** — from agent-reach. The installer is a markdown runbook an LLM agent executes: thin 'For Humans' one-line trigger over an imperative 'For AI Agents' program. Hard boundaries (no sudo, confined to ~/.agent-reach/, never pollute the workspace), env-branched steps (PEP 668 / Homebrew / Windows Store-alias), explicit user checkpoints, 'selector/router, never a wrapper'. install.md / update.md / daily watch = one lifecycle mechanism.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/agent-distribution/study/agent-driven-install--from-agent-reach.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/agent-distribution/build/agent-driven-install--from-agent-reach.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/Panniantong/Agent-Reach)

- **Self-Customizing CRM via Claude Code Commands** — from auto-crm. The configuration layer is an AI agent: .claude/commands/*.md runbooks let Claude edit the real Drizzle schema/components to fit the business instead of a settings UI. Structural vs operational commands, guard-railed by CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/agent-distribution/study/self-customizing-crm--from-auto-crm.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/agent-distribution/build/self-customizing-crm--from-auto-crm.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/Hainrixz/auto-crm)

### Ai Automation (2)
How software turns natural-language user intent into structured, automated actions over a stream of incoming items — with an LLM in the loop for the judgment calls and deterministic code for everything that can be decided cheaply.

- **AI Rules Engine** — from inbox-zero. Plain-English inbox automation. Cost funnel: static/pattern matching first (address-anchored, AND/OR), LLM rule-selection only for the ambiguous remainder (Zod schema + 'no match' flag), second LLM call fills {{template}} action args, deterministic executor runs actions + writes ExecutedRule/ExecutedAction audit trail. Dry-run mode skips all writes.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/ai-automation/study/ai-rules-engine--from-inbox-zero.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/ai-automation/build/ai-rules-engine--from-inbox-zero.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/elie222/inbox-zero)

- **AI Reply Drafting** — from inbox-zero. Tone-matched reply drafts + confidence (LOW/MED/HIGH). Assembles thread (per-msg ~3000 char cap), profile, knowledge, calendar slots, past drafts. Writing-style priority: explicit > learned > default. Learned style is a distilled PII-free summary (<=1500 chars) of the user's edits. Output normalizer + repetition guard.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/ai-automation/study/ai-reply-drafting--from-inbox-zero.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/ai-automation/build/ai-reply-drafting--from-inbox-zero.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/elie222/inbox-zero)

### Data Structures (2)
Foundational data representations that make higher-level features possible.

- **MBQL — Metabase Query AST** — from metabase. A database-agnostic query as a typed nested data structure (EDN/JSON) formally specified by a Malli schema: {:database,:type,:query} with tagged-vector clauses ([:field id opts], [:sum field], [:= lhs rhs]). Everything downstream rewrites the query as DATA; only the driver compiles to a dialect. Legacy + MBQL-5, normalized to one.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/data-structures/study/mbql-query-ast--from-metabase.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/data-structures/build/mbql-query-ast--from-metabase.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/metabase/metabase)

- **Fractional Indexing (z-order)** — from excalidraw. String order keys (base-62) where any two have a key between them, so insert/move/reorder mutates ONLY the moved element. orderByFractionalIndex sorts by string compare (id tiebreak). Invariant predecessor<current<successor with detect (validateFractionalIndices) + repair of contiguous broken runs (syncInvalidIndices). JITTERED fork of generateNKeysBetween so concurrent same-gap inserts don't collide.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/data-structures/study/fractional-indexing--from-excalidraw.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/data-structures/build/fractional-indexing--from-excalidraw.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw)

### Deployment (2)
Patterns for deploying locally-built, in-browser, or AI-generated apps to cloud hosting providers — including token validation, framework auto-detection, file upload, environment variable pre-configuration, and deployment status polling.

- **One-Click Deployment** — from bolt-diy. Server routes for Vercel (v9 project API + v13 deploy API, 2-min poll) and Netlify (SHA-1 content-addressable hash protocol, 60s poll, 3-retry file upload). Framework detection from package.json deps + config files. Project IDs cached client-side for re-deploys.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/deployment/study/one-click-deployment--from-bolt-diy.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/deployment/build/one-click-deployment--from-bolt-diy.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/stackblitz-labs/bolt.diy)

- **Cloud Deploy** — from dyad. One-click Vercel deployment from a local Electron builder. Token validation, framework auto-detection (config files then package.json deps), project create via REST API, env var sync (Neon/Supabase) before first build, deployment status polling for live URL.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/deployment/study/cloud-deploy--from-dyad.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/deployment/build/cloud-deploy--from-dyad.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/dyad-sh/dyad)

### Graph Editing (2)
The interactions that change the graph itself — drawing a new edge by dragging from one node's handle to another, and resizing a node by dragging its border.

- **Connection Handles** — from xyflow. Drag-to-connect with no d3: raw pointer listeners on document/shadow-host. Closest-handle within radius BUT DOM elementFromPoint wins; strict/loose validity + self-loop block + custom predicate; onConnect on valid drop.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/graph-editing/study/connection-handles--from-xyflow.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/graph-editing/build/connection-handles--from-xyflow.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/xyflow/xyflow)

- **Node Resizer** — from xyflow. d3-drag on 8 control points. Corner-anchoring (top/left handles move x/y to pin the opposite edge), aspect captured at start, min/max + parent/child extents, children shifted inverse to origin move, emits only on real change.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/graph-editing/study/node-resizer--from-xyflow.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/graph-editing/build/node-resizer--from-xyflow.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/xyflow/xyflow)

### Inbox Cleanup (2)
unsubscribing from unwanted senders, mass-archiving low-value mail, and tracking per-sender status so the system stops re-bothering the user.

- **Bulk Archiver** — from inbox-zero. Risk-tiered archive triage. Pure function scoring senders by category-name substring: HIGH (marketing/promotion/newsletter/sale), MEDIUM (notification/alert/receipt/update), LOW (other). Returns {confidence, reason} candidates. Only HIGH is one-click — tiering is the safety mechanism. Scoring decoupled from execution.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/inbox-cleanup/study/bulk-archiver--from-inbox-zero.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/inbox-cleanup/build/bulk-archiver--from-inbox-zero.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/elie222/inbox-zero)

- **Bulk Unsubscriber** — from inbox-zero. Real server-side one-click unsubscribe. Parses RFC 2369 List-Unsubscribe (extracts https URL), RFC 8058 one-click POST (body 'List-Unsubscribe=One-Click') with GET fallback, SSRF-guarded fetch, <=5 redirects with 301/302/303->GET downgrade. Persists per-sender Newsletter status (APPROVED/UNSUBSCRIBED/AUTO_ARCHIVED).
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/inbox-cleanup/study/bulk-unsubscriber--from-inbox-zero.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/inbox-cleanup/build/bulk-unsubscriber--from-inbox-zero.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/elie222/inbox-zero)

### Macos Ui (2)
Native macOS desktop-integration patterns — owning unusual window real estate, drawing above the system UI, and taking over system inputs/indicators — usually by reaching past public AppKit into private window-server, CoreGraphics, and event APIs.

- **Notch-Shaped Always-On-Top Window** — from boring-notch. Borderless non-activating NSPanel shaped to the MacBook notch, pinned above everything across Spaces & fullscreen via a private CGSSpace at Int32.max (flag=0x1 mandatory), plus a SkyLight-framework path for lock-screen visibility. Sizing from NSScreen.auxiliaryTopArea (width) + safeAreaInsets.top (height). Private CGS/SkyLight APIs via @_silgen_name and dlopen.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/macos-ui/study/notch-shaped-window--from-boring-notch.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/macos-ui/build/notch-shaped-window--from-boring-notch.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/TheBoredTeam/boring.notch)

- **System HUD Replacement** — from boring-notch. CGEventTap at .cghidEventTap (.defaultTap) intercepting NX_SYSDEFINED(14) media keys; suppresses Apple's OSD by returning nil (consuming the event), then sets the value itself — volume via public CoreAudio (kAudioDevicePropertyVolumeScalar/Mute), brightness/backlight via private API in an XPC helper — and shows a custom auto-hiding notch HUD. Needs Accessibility permission.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/macos-ui/study/system-hud-replacement--from-boring-notch.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/macos-ui/build/system-hud-replacement--from-boring-notch.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/TheBoredTeam/boring.notch)

### Realtime (2)
real-time multiplayer collaboration — letting many clients edit shared state at once with instant local feedback, sane conflict resolution, and graceful recovery. The interesting engineering lives in the conflict model (CRDT vs.

- **Multiplayer Sync** — from tldraw. Server-authoritative optimistic-rebase sync over WebSockets. Clients edit locally, stash speculative changes, push NetworkDiffs with a clientClock; server applies, assigns serverClock, returns commit/discard/rebase and broadcasts patches. Clients rebase (rewind→apply→replay). Presence synced-not-persisted; tombstone catch-up; per-session schema migration. Reference server = Cloudflare Durable Object.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/realtime/study/multiplayer-sync--from-tldraw.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/realtime/build/multiplayer-sync--from-tldraw.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/tldraw/tldraw)

- **Debounced File Watcher** — from hazelnut. Watches dirs via the OS notify API, polls a channel non-blockingly, debounces by last-seen-time per path (cap+cleanup), acts only on Create/Modify, routes each file to its watched dir's allowed rule set via longest-prefix canonical matching, and runs a background initial scan of existing files. Skips symlinks + NotFound races.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/realtime/study/debounced-file-watcher--from-hazelnut.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/realtime/build/debounced-file-watcher--from-hazelnut.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/ricardodantas/hazelnut)

### Realtime Collab (2)
how concurrent edits sync, merge, and persist while staying consistent across clients — and, where it matters, private from the server.

- **Scene Reconciliation** — from excalidraw. CRDT-free deterministic merge of local+remote element lists. Per-element last-write-wins: version is the logical clock, versionNonce the tiebreaker (lowest wins on equal version, using <= for a total relation). Active-editing (editing/resizing/new) guards override version math. Sorts + repairs fractional z-index last. Same fn runs client-side and inside the Firebase save transaction.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/realtime-collab/study/scene-reconciliation--from-excalidraw.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/realtime-collab/build/scene-reconciliation--from-excalidraw.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw)

- **E2E-Encrypted Collaboration** — from excalidraw. Zero-knowledge collab: AES-GCM 128 room key lives in the URL fragment (#room=ID,KEY) so the server never sees it; fresh 12-byte IV per message. socket.io relay only routes ciphertext. Delta broadcast via version Map + periodic full resync; reliable edits vs volatile presence. Encrypted Firebase persistence {sceneVersion,iv,ciphertext}, debounced, transactional reconcile on save.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/realtime-collab/study/e2e-encrypted-collaboration--from-excalidraw.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/realtime-collab/build/e2e-encrypted-collaboration--from-excalidraw.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw)

### Runtime (2)
In-browser and sandboxed code execution environments. Features in this domain cover running real server-side code (Node.js, Python, etc.

- **WebContainer Runtime** — from bolt-diy. Boots a full Node.js runtime in the browser tab using @webcontainer/api. Writes files via wc.fs.writeFile(), runs npm/node via wc.spawn(), serves live HTTP previews from the container. Requires COEP+COOP headers. Singleton per tab via import.meta.hot.data.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/runtime/study/webcontainer-runtime--from-bolt-diy.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/runtime/build/webcontainer-runtime--from-bolt-diy.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/stackblitz-labs/bolt.diy)

- **Unified Runtimes + CLI Auto-Detection** — from multica. Local daemon auto-detects 11+ agent CLIs by PATH probe (env-overridable), registers each as a runtime, and wraps each behind a backend that normalizes its native stream (stream-json/app-server/acp/json) into uniform task events. Binary-name traps: cursor-agent, kiro-cli.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/runtime/study/unified-runtimes-cli-detection--from-multica.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/runtime/build/unified-runtimes-cli-detection--from-multica.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/multica-ai/multica)

### Streaming (2)
Real-time stream output, transcoding, re-muxing, and multi-destination delivery.

- **Stream Output & Transcoding** — from vlc. Declarative stream_out chain: '#transcode{vcodec=h264,vb=800,acodec=mp3}:http{mux=ts,dst=:8080/}'. Transcode module decodes→filters→re-encodes per track; duplicate fans to N outputs. VLM API manages named broadcasts. PCR-aware for MPEG-TS. Hardware encoders (NVENC/VideoToolbox) drop-in via module system.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/streaming/study/stream-output-transcoding--from-vlc.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/streaming/build/stream-output-transcoding--from-vlc.md) · [origin repo](https://code.videolan.org/videolan/vlc)

- **Resumable Streaming Search** — from scira. Decouple durable generation (consumeStream + after(), buffered to Redis via ai-resumable-stream) from reconnectable delivery. GET resumes by replaying Redis chunks or returns the just-saved message within 15s; DELETE aborts via a shared AbortController. Graceful non-resumable fallback if no Redis.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/streaming/study/resumable-streaming-search--from-scira.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/streaming/build/resumable-streaming-search--from-scira.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/zaidmukaddam/scira)

### Activity Tracking (1)
Logging every interaction with a record (calls, emails, meetings, notes, scheduled follow-ups) as a timeline, and triaging the pending ones by urgency so nothing falls through the cracks.

- **Activity Tracking & Follow-up Buckets** — from auto-crm. Activities timeline; pending follow-up = scheduledAt set & completedAt null. Buckets incomplete items into overdue/today/upcoming/unscheduled via Unix-seconds day-window math. Backs web board + MCP crm_get_followups; feeds scoring recency.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/activity-tracking/study/followup-buckets--from-auto-crm.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/activity-tracking/build/followup-buckets--from-auto-crm.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/Hainrixz/auto-crm)

### Adaptive Parsing (1)
Features that make data extraction survive change — selectors, parsers, or extractors that don't break the moment a source (a website, a document format, an API response shape) is restructured.

- **Adaptive Element Relocation** — from scrapling. Fingerprints an element (tag/text/attrs/siblings/path/parent) on first scrape, then re-finds it by fuzzy similarity scoring when the selector later breaks. Default 40% threshold. SQLite-backed, pluggable storage.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/adaptive-parsing/study/adaptive-element-relocation--from-scrapling.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/adaptive-parsing/build/adaptive-element-relocation--from-scrapling.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/D4Vinci/Scrapling)

### Agent Guardrails (1)
Mechanisms that constrain what an AI coding agent is allowed to produce — not by asking it nicely in a prompt, but by deterministic enforcement that runs outside the model and can block its output.

- **Deterministic Security Hooks** — from pagokit. Enforce rules on an AI agent's output via Claude Code hooks: PreToolUse/PostToolUse/Stop on Write|Edit|MultiEdit -> one dispatcher -> pluggable run(ctx)->finding|null checks. Exit 2 blocks the write; fail-open on crashes/timeouts. Comment/string stripping + test allowlist + pagokit-ignore bypass + audit log. Domain-agnostic guardrail engine.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/agent-guardrails/study/deterministic-security-hooks--from-pagokit.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/agent-guardrails/build/deterministic-security-hooks--from-pagokit.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/Hainrixz/agente-pagokit)

### Analytics (1)
Turning stored business data into at-a-glance situational awareness — KPI scoreboards and charts that answer "what's the shape of things right now?" without a query or an export.

- **CRM Dashboard KPIs & Analytics** — from auto-crm. KPI cards (contacts, active deals, pipeline value, conversion) + Recharts breakdowns. One server-side getStats() reused by web UI and MCP crm_get_stats so numbers never drift; won/lost via stage flags not names; shared color tokens.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/analytics/study/crm-dashboard-kpis--from-auto-crm.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/analytics/build/crm-dashboard-kpis--from-auto-crm.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/Hainrixz/auto-crm)

### App Distribution (1)
Getting a desktop app, and its future updates, onto users' machines: signing releases, publishing a feed, and letting the installed app update itself safely.

- **Sparkle EdDSA Auto-Update** — from open-caffeine. Self-updating macOS app via Sparkle with EdDSA-signed releases. SPUStandardUpdaterController + Info.plist (SUFeedURL to a raw-GitHub appcast.xml, SUPublicEDKey). A release script builds, zips with ditto, and runs generate_appcast to EdDSA-sign; the pushed appcast.xml is the feed and binaries live on GitHub Releases. Zero update server.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/app-distribution/study/sparkle-eddsa-autoupdate--from-open-caffeine.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/app-distribution/build/sparkle-eddsa-autoupdate--from-open-caffeine.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/sapsaldog/open-caffeine)

### App Management (1)
Patterns for managing a portfolio of generated or user-created projects within a single builder tool — including project scaffolding, process lifecycle, collections/grouping, commit-keyed thumbnails, bulk operations, and multi-backend file search.

- **Multi-App Library** — from dyad. Multi-project portfolio in a local builder: SQLite app+collection schema, template scaffold with git init, child-process lifecycle map, commit-keyed PNG thumbnails with LRU pruning, soft-deleted collections, ripgrep content search with byte-to-char offset conversion.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/app-management/study/multi-app-library--from-dyad.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/app-management/build/multi-app-library--from-dyad.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/dyad-sh/dyad)

### Code Intelligence (1)
Tools and infrastructure that give AI agents (or humans) semantic, symbol-level understanding of a codebase — going beyond raw text search to understand what code means: what a symbol is, where it's defined, what references it, what its type hierarchy is, and how to edit it safely.

- **Semantic Symbol Tools** — from serena. MCP-exposed LSP-backed toolkit for AI agents: find/navigate/edit code symbols by name-path (ClassName/method), replace bodies, insert before/after, rename across codebase, safe-delete, pull diagnostics — no line numbers needed, 40+ languages.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/code-intelligence/study/semantic-symbol-tools--from-serena.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/code-intelligence/build/semantic-symbol-tools--from-serena.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/oraios/serena)

### Diagnostics (1)
Patterns for answering "is this actually working right now?" — health probing of external dependencies, survivable aggregation into a human/agent-readable report, and turning failures into actionable, specific fixes.

- **Channel Health Diagnostics** — from agent-reach. probe_command EXECUTES a side-effect-free command to classify missing/broken(stale venv shebang, exit 126/127)/timeout/ok — which() existence is not health. doctor aggregates per-channel (exceptions degrade to error, stale active_backend cleared), groups by tier, annotates the active backend only when >1 exists, and audits config.yaml permissions (chmod 600). Retries only transient failures.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/diagnostics/study/channel-health-diagnostics--from-agent-reach.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/diagnostics/build/channel-health-diagnostics--from-agent-reach.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/Panniantong/Agent-Reach)

### Durability (1)
Making an in-memory database survive process crashes and restarts: write-ahead logging of every change, periodic full snapshots, and a recovery procedure that replays them to reconstruct state.

- **WAL + Snapshot Durability** — from memgraph. Crash-safety for an in-memory store: append-only WAL of 40+ delta variants framed per-transaction with a deferred commit-flag patch for atomicity + per-txn CRC; sectioned snapshots with an offset table for seek-based load; recovery = newest snapshot then replay later WALs; versioned format (kVersion 36) with composable version-dependent decoders; retention keeps N snapshots + bridging WALs.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/durability/study/wal-snapshot-durability--from-memgraph.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/durability/build/wal-snapshot-durability--from-memgraph.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/memgraph/memgraph)

### Email Platform (1)
a single, provider-agnostic interface that wraps Gmail and Outlook/Microsoft Graph behind one set of methods, plus the OAuth token plumbing that keeps those clients alive.

- **Email Provider Abstraction** — from inbox-zero. Adapter+factory seam over Gmail + Outlook/Graph. One EmailProvider interface, GmailProvider/OutlookProvider adapters normalize to a shared ParsedMessage. createEmailProvider validates provider, fails fast on rate-limit, gets OAuth client (token refresh hidden in client getters). Label vs folder absorbed by adapters.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/email-platform/study/email-provider-abstraction--from-inbox-zero.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/email-platform/build/email-provider-abstraction--from-inbox-zero.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/elie222/inbox-zero)

### File Detection (1)
Determining a file's true type from content, extension, MIME type hints, and ML-based identification — without trusting the filename alone.

- **Magika File Detection** — from markitdown. Layered type detection: extension+MIME hints → mimetypes bidirectional enrichment → Magika ML content scan → charset_normalizer for text files. Returns ranked StreamInfo list.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/file-detection/study/magika-file-detection--from-markitdown.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/file-detection/build/magika-file-detection--from-markitdown.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/microsoft/markitdown)

### Fuzzy Search (1)
typos, prefixes, and near-misses, so a search finds what the user meant, not only what they typed exactly.

- **Length-Gated Typo Tolerance** — from meilisearch. Typo tolerance with the typo budget gated by word length (0 typos for short words, then 1 then 2 as words lengthen; thresholds are index settings), an exact-words FST override, and last-word prefix expansion for search-as-you-type. A query word becomes a set of derivations looked up in the inverted index, each carrying its typo cost so exact matches still rank first.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/fuzzy-search/study/length-gated-typo-tolerance--from-meilisearch.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/fuzzy-search/build/length-gated-typo-tolerance--from-meilisearch.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/meilisearch/meilisearch)

### Git (1)
Git operations that run inside the browser without a local git CLI.

- **Browser Git** — from bolt-diy. isomorphic-git clone in the browser routed through a server-side Remix CORS proxy that adds Access-Control-Allow-Origin: * to git server responses. Shallow clone (depth:1), binary file exclusion via TextDecoder fatal:true, LightningFS in-memory storage. detectProjectCommands() seeds the chat.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/git/study/browser-git--from-bolt-diy.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/git/build/browser-git--from-bolt-diy.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/stackblitz-labs/bolt.diy)

### Graph Rendering (1)
The pure geometry that turns "two points with facing directions" into a drawable SVG path between nodes — bezier curves, orthogonal step routes with rounded corners, and straight lines — plus where to place the edge label.

- **Edge Path Algorithms** — from xyflow. Pure dependency-free SVG path fns: getBezierPath (curvature*25*sqrt(-d) loop trick, t=0.5 label weights), getSmoothStepPath (orthogonal route, corner radius clamped to half-segment), getStraightPath. Returns [path,labelX,labelY,offsetX,offsetY].
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/graph-rendering/study/edge-path-algorithms--from-xyflow.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/graph-rendering/build/edge-path-algorithms--from-xyflow.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/xyflow/xyflow)

### Lead Ingestion (1)
Getting leads/contacts into the system from the outside world — web forms, automation tools, batch files — with as little friction as possible and tolerant of messy, inconsistent payloads.

- **Webhook Lead Ingestion** — from auto-crm. Public POST endpoint mapping multilingual/Typeform field aliases -> contact. Optional x-webhook-secret auth, name required, defaults webhook/cold/0. No dedup, no scoring on ingest (by design); echoes received keys on 400.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/lead-ingestion/study/webhook-lead-ingestion--from-auto-crm.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/lead-ingestion/build/webhook-lead-ingestion--from-auto-crm.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/Hainrixz/auto-crm)

### Lead Scoring (1)
Turning a pile of leads/contacts into a ranked, triaged queue so a salesperson knows who to work first — via a transparent score and a coarse temperature tier.

- **Rule-Based Lead Scoring** — from auto-crm. Deterministic 0-100 lead score: temperature base + contact completeness + capped engagement - recency decay + deal-value bonus, clamped; thresholds 70/40 -> hot/warm/cold. Offline, pure, doubles as the AI classifier's fallback.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/lead-scoring/study/rule-based-lead-scoring--from-auto-crm.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/lead-scoring/build/rule-based-lead-scoring--from-auto-crm.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/Hainrixz/auto-crm)

### Media Control (1)
Patterns for reading and controlling media/now-playing playback across heterogeneous sources behind a single unified interface — the adapter pattern applied to music/media players with wildly different control surfaces.

- **Multi-Provider Media Control** — from boring-notch. Unified now-playing across Apple Music/Spotify (AppleScript), YouTube Music (local HTTP+WebSocket@26538), and system NowPlaying (private MediaRemote read via a Perl DynaLoader subprocess, commands via MediaRemote.framework in-process). One MediaControllerProtocol publishing a normalized PlaybackState; MusicManager selects+hot-swaps the active adapter; capability flags + deprecation fallback.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/media-control/study/multi-provider-media-control--from-boring-notch.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/media-control/build/multi-provider-media-control--from-boring-notch.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/TheBoredTeam/boring.notch)

### Messaging (1)
Patterns for connecting an app to chat/messaging channels (WhatsApp, SMS, Telegram, etc.) through a provider-agnostic abstraction, so the core logic never depends on which vendor delivers the messages.

- **WhatsApp Provider Adapter Layer** — from whatsapp-agentkit. Adapter/strategy pattern for a messaging channel: abstract ProveedorWhatsApp + env-driven factory + Meta/Twilio adapters normalizing webhooks into a common MensajeEntrante. Core code never branches on provider; base carries a no-op validar_webhook only Meta overrides; lazy-imports the chosen adapter.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/messaging/study/whatsapp-provider-adapter--from-whatsapp-agentkit.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/messaging/build/whatsapp-provider-adapter--from-whatsapp-agentkit.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/Hainrixz/whatsapp-agentkit)

### Onboarding (1)
the moments between "app just launched for the first time" and "user understands what this thing is and has granted it everything it needs to work.

- **First-Launch Video Onboarding** — from clicky. First run is gated by a UserDefaults 'hasCompletedOnboarding' flag; the menu-bar panel primes and requests four macOS permissions (Microphone, Accessibility, Screen Recording, Screen Content) with native-prompt-then-Settings fallbacks, then on Start plays an aha-moment where a blue companion cursor types 'hey! i'm clicky' and a remote Mux HLS demo video (AVPlayer) fades in beside the live cursor. Replayable.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/onboarding/study/first-launch-video-onboarding--from-clicky.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/onboarding/build/first-launch-video-onboarding--from-clicky.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/farzaa/clicky)

### Permissions (1)
How apps ask for, check, and recover user consent to sensitive capabilities (camera, location, contacts, health, notifications, …) — normalized into one uniform model regardless of the wildly inconsistent native APIs underneath.

- **Unified Permission Abstraction** — from permissionskit. Abstract Permission base class normalizing 18 Apple-platform permissions onto a 4-state status model (authorized/denied/notDetermined/notSupported) + uniform request/openSettingPage; sub-flavors carried as associated values on a Kind enum.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/permissions/study/unified-permission-abstraction--from-permissionskit.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/permissions/build/unified-permission-abstraction--from-permissionskit.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/sparrowcode/PermissionsKit)

### Persistence (1)
Client-side and server-side storage strategies for AI-powered applications: chat history, conversation snapshots, project state, and metadata.

- **Chat Persistence (IndexedDB)** — from bolt-diy. IndexedDB 'boltHistory' v2 with chats store (id+urlId indexes, messages, metadata) and snapshots store (chatId key, file tree). Supports fork-at-message, duplicate, delete. Deployment IDs in metadata.netlifySiteId/vercelProjectId for re-deploys. Context summaries stored as chatSummary-typed messages.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/persistence/study/chat-persistence--from-bolt-diy.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/persistence/build/chat-persistence--from-bolt-diy.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/stackblitz-labs/bolt.diy)

### Power Management (1)
Controlling the host operating system's power and sleep behaviour from an app: holding the machine (or just the display) awake, releasing cleanly, and tying that to timers, battery state, and user intent.

- **Power-Assertion Wake Lock** — from open-caffeine. Prevent macOS idle sleep via IOKit power assertions behind a testable PowerAssertionAPI seam. displayAndSystem vs systemOnly assertion types; a timed session (Timer) auto-releases on expiry; a battery-threshold monitor (IOPS power sources) ends the session on low battery; deinit always releases.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/power-management/study/power-assertion-wake-lock--from-open-caffeine.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/power-management/build/power-assertion-wake-lock--from-open-caffeine.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/sapsaldog/open-caffeine)

### Privacy (1)
Patterns for protecting user data at every layer — capture-time exclusion, pattern-based secret redaction before storage or AI processing, encryption at rest with OS-managed keys, and audit controls that give users visibility and control over what is retained.

- **Pattern-Based Secret Redaction** — from asyar. multi-pattern regex pipeline that intercepts clipboard content, AI context, and user input before storage, redacting API keys, JWTs, credit cards, and secrets in place; AES-256-GCM encryption at rest with OS keychain key management
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/privacy/study/pattern-based-secret-redaction--from-asyar.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/privacy/build/pattern-based-secret-redaction--from-asyar.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/Xoshbin/asyar)

### Reactivity (1)
fine-grained reactive state systems — the machinery that makes derived values and side-effects update automatically and surgically when their inputs change, without the developer wiring dependencies by hand.

- **Signals Reactivity Engine** — from tldraw. Fine-grained reactivity (atom/computed/react) coordinated by a single global epoch counter. Dependencies auto-discovered by observing reads; staleness decided by epoch comparison, not value diffing. Signals carry diff-history so consumers can ask 'what changed since epoch N?'. Zero deps.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/reactivity/study/signals-reactivity-engine--from-tldraw.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/reactivity/build/signals-reactivity-engine--from-tldraw.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/tldraw/tldraw)

### Schema Migrations (1)
evolving a persisted data format over time without breaking existing data — and, in the hardest cases, without breaking communication between clients on different versions.

- **Schema & Migrations** — from tldraw. Versioned bidirectional migrations. Namespaced ids (sequenceId/version) grouped into sequences with record/store/storage scope; cross-sequence dependsOn resolved by topological sort. Compact SerializedSchema travels with snapshots + connect handshake; up to upgrade, down to serve older peers.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/schema-migrations/study/schema-migrations--from-tldraw.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/schema-migrations/build/schema-migrations--from-tldraw.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/tldraw/tldraw)

### Search Relevance (1)
turning a candidate set of matching documents into a ranked list using a defined, multi-stage policy rather than a single score.

- **Ranking-Rules Bucket Sort** — from meilisearch. Order search results by a fixed, reorderable cascade of ranking rules (words, typo, proximity, attribute, sort, exactness) applied as a bucket sort over RoaringBitmap candidate sets. Each rule breaks ties left by the previous and only sorts as deep as the requested page; typo/proximity are cheapest-path costs over a query-term graph.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/search-relevance/study/ranking-rules-bucket-sort--from-meilisearch.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/search-relevance/build/ranking-rules-bucket-sort--from-meilisearch.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/meilisearch/meilisearch)

### Storage Engine (1)
The in-process engine that holds a database's records in memory and governs how concurrent transactions read and mutate them — versioning, visibility, isolation, and garbage collection.

- **In-Memory MVCC Storage Engine** — from memgraph. In-memory vertex/edge store: per-object delta chains walked backward by timestamp for 3 isolation levels; 56-byte trivially-destructible Delta; pointer-tagging packs type+flags free; non-sequential edge deltas relax MVCC for fast bulk import; lock-free reads, per-object lock only for chain mutation; commit = one atomic timestamp store.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/storage-engine/study/mvcc-inmemory-storage-engine--from-memgraph.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/storage-engine/build/mvcc-inmemory-storage-engine--from-memgraph.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/memgraph/memgraph)

### Task Scheduling (1)
queueing them, deciding which can be combined, and processing them in efficient batches with durable status.

- **Auto-Batching Write Scheduler** — from meilisearch. Coalesce a stream of write tasks into efficient batches: classify each by kind, greedily merge the longest compatible prefix of consecutive same-index tasks into one batch (a compatibility matrix, not a timer), process atomically in one transaction, and keep durable per-task status. Structural ops like index delete/swap break the batch.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/task-scheduling/study/auto-batching-write-scheduler--from-meilisearch.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/task-scheduling/build/auto-batching-write-scheduler--from-meilisearch.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/meilisearch/meilisearch)

### Testing Discipline (1)
structuring code so the logic is testable, and enforcing a coverage bar automatically so it can't rot.

- **Coverage-Gated Testable Core** — from open-caffeine. 100% line-coverage gate over a curated logic core. Decision logic is extracted out of UI/system shells into pure types + protocol seams; thin OS-touching impls go in a justified, self-policing EXCLUDE list; a pre-push hook runs xccov and fails the push if any non-excluded file is below 100%. New files are not exempt by default.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/testing-discipline/study/coverage-gated-testable-core--from-open-caffeine.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/testing-discipline/build/coverage-gated-testable-core--from-open-caffeine.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/sapsaldog/open-caffeine)

### Transcription (1)
Turning spoken audio in video/audio media into clean, timestamped text — preferring free existing captions, falling back to paid speech-to-text APIs only when needed.

- **Captions-First Transcription Cascade** — from claude-video. Cost-ordered transcript: parse yt-dlp WebVTT captions (deduping YouTube rolling duplicates) first/free; else extract tiny 16kHz mono mp3 and POST to Groq whisper-large-v3 / OpenAI whisper-1 via hand-rolled stdlib multipart (no SDK) with retry/backoff. Non-default UA dodges Groq's Cloudflare WAF. All sources normalize to {start,end,text}.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/transcription/study/captions-first-transcription-cascade--from-claude-video.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/transcription/build/captions-first-transcription-cascade--from-claude-video.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/bradautomates/claude-video)

### Vector Search (1)
Approximate-nearest-neighbour (ANN) similarity search over high-dimensional embeddings, embedded inside a primary datastore so semantic retrieval lives next to the records it indexes. The retrieval half of GraphRAG and AI-memory systems.

- **USearch Vector Index** — from memgraph. Embedded HNSW ANN index (USearch) over node/edge embeddings, key = vertex pointer; per-index metric/dimension/scalar-kind/capacity; SINGLE/WILDCARD/ANY_OF/ALL_OF membership filter; copy-on-write index registry for snapshot-stable reads; READ_UNCOMMITTED safe under UNIQUE host access; undo-able OOM-protected drop; serialized into snapshots + WAL create/drop recovery.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/vector-search/study/usearch-vector-index--from-memgraph.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/vector-search/build/usearch-vector-index--from-memgraph.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/memgraph/memgraph)

### Web Cloning (1)
Reverse-engineering a live website — its visual design, interactions, assets, and layout — into a clean, production-grade codebase by combining browser-based reconnaissance, AI-generated component specifications, and parallel multi-agent code construction.

- **Website Cloning Pipeline** — from ai-website-cloner-template. 5-phase AI pipeline (recon → foundation → component specs → parallel build → assemble+QA) that converts a target URL into a deployable Next.js 16/React 19 app. Uses getComputedStyle() for pixel-accurate design extraction, git worktrees for parallel agent builds, and spec-driven component construction.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/web-cloning/study/website-cloning-pipeline--from-ai-website-cloner-template.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/web-cloning/build/website-cloning-pipeline--from-ai-website-cloner-template.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/JCodesMore/ai-website-cloner-template)

## Relationship types
- depends-on (162): one pattern needs another to work
- same-repo (210): shipped in the same project
- same-domain (55): solves a related problem
- similar-pattern (84): an independent take on the same idea
- alternative-to (22): a competing approach

## Convergent solutions (same problem, different repos, worth comparing)
